title: Software Engineer — Medical Imaging & Treatment Planning
about: ASPL Fusion (Agnira Sanlayan Pvt. Ltd.) is an applied plasma and neutron-systems company working on accelerator-based BNCT, domestic medical-isotope production, and industrial neutron applications, within established Indian regulatory and safety frameworks. The IGRT (Image-Guided Radiation Therapy) Treatment Planning System is the software that plans BNCT treatments — DICOM image ingestion, dose-engine integration, 3-D visualisation, and the clinical workflow tools that wrap them.
description: Architect and build the BNCT treatment-planning system. Greenfield software development across DICOM image processing, 3-D visualisation, dose-engine integration, and clinical workflow tools. Work at the intersection of medical imaging, scientific computing, and clinical software.
responsibilities: Architect and develop the BNCT treatment-planning software
Implement dose-calculation algorithms (Monte Carlo–based neutron dose in tissue)
Build 3-D patient visualisation (CT / MRI data, organ segmentation, dose overlays)
Integrate boron-concentration imaging (PET-based BPA distribution mapping)
Ensure DICOM compliance for hospital integration
Validate dose accuracy against phantom measurements with the medical-physics team
requirements: B.Tech / M.Tech in Computer Science, Biomedical Engineering, or equivalent
2+ years building medical-imaging or scientific-visualisation software
Experience with the DICOM standard and medical image processing
Strong 3-D graphics / visualisation skills (VTK, ITK, Three.js, or equivalent)
preferred: Experience with radiation-therapy TPS systems (Eclipse, RayStation, or equivalent)
GPU computing (CUDA / OpenCL) for Monte Carlo acceleration
Familiarity with IEC 62304 or FDA software validation requirements
benefits: Co-authorship on ASPL publications and patent filings (the default for technical roles)
Conference travel support (ANS, IEEE NPSS, IPAC, ASTRO, IAEA technical meetings and equivalent)
Direct mentorship from Prof. Prabhat Ranjan and Prof. Y.C. Saxena, plus weekly working-group technical syncs
Founding-team scope: shape your domain end-to-end as part of a small team scaling up
Core collaboration hours 10:00–18:00 IST with flexibility outside that window
